title: T-119 — Pending Invitations screen (SET-010)
prompt:
# T-119 — Pending Invitations screen (SET-010)

## Goal
SET-010 Pending Invitations production screen, in scope per PO 2026-05-03.

Canonical Settings prototype source of truth is `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/*.jsx`. If an exact screen prototype is missing, implement spec-driven from `prototypes/design/02-SETTINGS-UX.md` and reuse available prototype elements/patterns only where they match the UX contract. Exact prototype for SET-010 is not confirmed; use UX SET-010 as authoritative and reuse user/invite table plus modal patterns from `prototypes/settings/access-screens.jsx` and `prototypes/settings/modals.jsx`.

## Files in scope
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/invitations/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/invitations/page.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given /settings/invitations renders, then it follows UX SET-010: table columns Email, Role, Invited By, Invited At, Expires At, Status, Actions; Pending/Expired/Accepted badges; Invite User trigger; loading and empty states.
- Given an invitation is Pending or Expired, when Resend is clicked, then the invite action is called and success/error toast is shown.
- Given an invitation is Pending, when Revoke is confirmed, then the invitation is revoked/audited and disappears or changes status without affecting accepted users.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: RTL page test for table columns/states and resend/revoke calls.
- GREEN: build screen wired to existing invite actions.
- Run page test, typecheck, lint.

## Risk red lines
- Do not fold Pending Invitations into /settings/users only; PO says Pending Invitations is in scope as its own Settings screen.
- Do not invent custom invite semantics outside T-017/T-052 contracts.

## Depth patch — UI permissions and invitation lifecycle
- Add route/nav visibility checks: `/settings/invitations` is visible only to users who can view users/invitations; write controls require invite/admin permissions.
- Distinguish actions by state: Pending supports Resend/Revoke; Expired supports Resend and optional cleanup; Accepted is read-only and must never revoke a real user.
- Show resend/revoke pending, success, error, and audit result states; include confirmation copy with invite email and expiry.
- Tests must cover permission-denied/read-only, all three statuses, loading/empty/error, and that accepted invitations cannot be revoked.

pipeline_inputs.description: SET-010 Pending Invitations production screen, in scope per PO 2026-05-03.
pipeline_inputs.details:
Canonical Settings prototype source of truth is `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/*.jsx`. If an exact screen prototype is missing, implement spec-driven from `prototypes/design/02-SETTINGS-UX.md` and reuse available prototype elements/patterns only where they match the UX contract. Exact prototype for SET-010 is not confirmed; use UX SET-010 as authoritative and reuse user/invite table plus modal patterns from `prototypes/settings/access-screens.jsx` and `prototypes/settings/modals.jsx`. Depth patch 2026-05-03: ## Depth patch — UI permissions and invitation lifecycle - Add route/nav visibility checks: `/settings/invitations` is visible only to users who can view users/invitations; write controls require invite/admin permissions. - Distinguish actions by state: Pending supports Resend/Revoke; Expired supports Resend and optional cleanup; Accepted is read-only and must never revoke a real user. - Show resend/revoke pending, success, error, and audit result states; include confirmation copy with invite email and expiry. - Tests must cover permission-denied/read-only, all three statuses, loading/empty/error, and that accepted invitations cannot be revoked.
Preserved acceptance requirements: Screen enforces `settings.users.invite`/`settings.roles.assign` permissions: users without invite permission see read-only table and no Invite/Resend/Revoke write controls.; Navigation/route access and write controls are permission-filtered; missing write permission renders a read-only table with no Invite/Resend/Revoke controls.; Accepted invitations are immutable/read-only; revoke never affects accepted users.; RTL tests cover pending, expired, accepted, loading, empty, error, and permission-denied states.

Additional acceptance detail preserved from compressed AC list:
- RTL covers pending, expired, accepted, loading, empty, error and permission-denied states.
- UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
acceptance_criteria: SET-010 invitations screen renders required table, badges, trigger, loading, empty and error states.
Pending/Expired actions call T-124 resend/revoke lifecycle and Accepted invitations are immutable/read-only.
Missing invite/role permissions render read-only table with no write controls.

Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect the task evidence, then these additional requirements are proven together: RTL covers pending, expired, accepted, loading, empty, error and permission-denied states.; UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: RED: RTL page test for table columns/states and resend/revoke calls.
GREEN: build screen wired to existing invite actions.
Add permission-denied/read-only test case for missing invite/admin permissions.
Add RTL permission-denied/read-only test, accepted-immutable test, and loading/empty/error state tests.
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Do not fold Pending Invitations into /settings/users only; PO says Pending Invitations is in scope as its own Settings screen.
Do not invent custom invite semantics outside T-017/T-052 contracts.
Do not expose resend/revoke controls to users lacking invite/admin permissions.
Do not allow revoke to mutate accepted users.
